silver.mention_text: Multiple 2-3 mm part solid and solid nodules (5, 44, 70, 71, 73, 101, 165, ___ in the left upper lobe, and lingula as well as right upper and lower lobes are indeterminate and may be related to
nodules are solid while majority of them are part solid the largest in the right lower lobe (3, 120 measuring 9 mm.
Subsolid nodules in the right upper lobe (4:98) measuring 1.2 x 1.1 cm, in the right lower lobe (4:73) measuring 2.0 x 1.2 cm and in the left lower lobe (4:147) measuring 1.0 cm.

A large number of 2-4 mm nodules of indeterminate nature, both ground-glass and solid density, is scattered throughout both lungs, for example right upper lobe, 03:27, 30, and left upper lobe, 4:89 but absent

The nodule in the superior segment of the right lower lobe has increased in size now measuring 10 mm previously 6 mm (02:40). Scattered ground-glass opacification is noted throughout the bilateral lungs with more prominently in the right lower lobe with a more focal area of ground-glass and septal thickening in the right lower lobe (2:86).
Scattered bilateral solid and ground-glass pulmonary nodules measuring up
Multiple sub-5-mm semisolid and ground-glass nodules with bilateral upper lobe predominance is nonspecific, but could represent Langerhans cell

Unchnaged is a right lower lobe nodule (3:46) measuring 4.2 mm on today's study. Fibrotic scarring at the left base and the lingula is also stable. New since ___ are several small nodular, centrilobular ground-glass
and a subpleural 5 mm right middle lobe lateral segment lung nodule (155, 4). Scattered smooth septal lines and ground-glass opacification suggests a
Interval increase in the solid component of a right upper lobe nodule measuring 5 mm, previously 2 mm is worrisome for development of adenocarcinoma
PARENCHYMA:  There is a 9 mm sub solid nodule in the right lower lobe
Bilateral calcified granulomas with additional sub 4 mm pulmonary nodules
Spiculated nodule in the right upper lobe is unchanged and measures 1.5 x 1.3
Additionally, there is a 0.8 cm spiculated nodule in the right middle lobe, which is highly concerning for neoplasm, although this finding is stable since ___.
calcified nodule, possibly a granuloma, unchanged. There are new patchy airspace and ground-glass opacities, predominately in the right lung,
lobe mixed solid and ground-glass nodule now measures 10 x 8 mm compared to 8
4 mm sub solid pulmonary nodule for which no follow-up is recommended per
